pattern: Membership testing is the whole problem: once each array's values live in a hash set, counting how many entries of the other array fall inside it is a single linear pass per direction.
hints: You need two counts, one for each direction -- how many entries of the first list also show up somewhere in the second, and vice versa.
Turn each array into a set first so 'does this value appear over there' becomes a constant-time lookup instead of a nested scan.
Walk nums1 once counting hits against the set built from nums2, then walk nums2 once counting hits against the set built from nums1, and return the two counts as a pair.

edge_inputs.description: single matching element in both arrays
no values shared between the two arrays
every value at the maximum allowed magnitude
repeated value in nums1 all matching a single occurrence in nums2, so the first count should reflect every repeat while the second stays small
repeated value in nums2 matching a single occurrence in nums1, mirroring the previous trap in the other direction
arrays share some values but each also holds values unique to itself

stress.description: random arrays near the maximum length drawn from the full value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100)))
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100)))
    nums1 = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    nums2 = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(m)]
    return [nums1, nums2]

stress.description: small arrays drawn from a narrow value range to force heavy overlap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100)))
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100)))
    nums1 = [rng.randint(1, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    nums2 = [rng.randint(1, 5) for _ in range(m)]
    return [nums1, nums2]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def findIntersectionValues(self, nums1, nums2):
        set1 = set(nums1)
        set2 = set(nums2)
        answer1 = sum(1 for x in nums1 if x in set2)
        answer2 = sum(1 for y in nums2 if y in set1)
        return [answer1, answer2]
